label: 232_egypt_egyptian_egypts_military aid
description: The U.S. considered releasing withheld arms to Egypt under President Sissi, amid domestic economic issues and political tensions. Testimony highlighted the need for a reset in the relationship based on outdated assumptions. Military aid was used as leverage over local populations in Sinai.

earliest_articles.title: How Obama Got Egypt Wrong
earliest_articles.excerpt: The following is Brookings Doha Center expert Shadi Hamid’s contribution to Pharaoh’s End, in which Foreign Policy magazine asked five top Egypt experts what President Obama should do in response to the nation’s protests. The Obama administration’s initial response to the ongoing Egyptian revolt was disappointing, but…

earliest_articles.title: In Egypt, the Time Has Come for Mubarak to Go
earliest_articles.excerpt: Events in the streets of Cairo and Egypt’s other cities are unfolding at a dizzying pace as the Egyptian people demand an end to the 30 year reign of Hosni Mubarak. In the past four days, the much-feared security police have lost control of the streets, the Egyptian army has entered the main squares but is embracing…

newest_articles.title: It’s time to take a hard look at the U.S. relationship with Egypt
newest_articles.excerpt: President Abdel Fatah el-Sissi of Egypt is celebrated in Washington as a key U.S. ally in counterterrorism efforts worldwide and an important supporter of U.S. efforts to bring peace and stability in the region. But he is neither, argue Robert Kagan and Michele Dunne. Egyptian President Abdel Fatah al-Sissi will be…

newest_articles.title: Egypt is Trump country
newest_articles.excerpt: What is behind President Trump’s embrace of Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sissi? Shadi Hamid explores, in a piece originally published by The Atlantic.
